title: Add email automation triggers based on From address and Subject line (with "contains" matching) in Monday Service
excerpt:

posts.author: dpg-emilyw (Emily Watts)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

Monday Service currently lacks basic email-based automation triggers that are standard in most ticketing platforms and even consumer email tools like Gmail. Specifically, there is no native way to create automations based on: (1) the From email address or domain of an incoming ticket, or (2) the Subject line of an incoming email — using partial/contains matching rather than exact match only.

Workarounds that rely on AI-powered Workflow steps to parse subject lines are overly complex for what should be simple, rule-based filtering. The Emails & Activities app does not currently expose these fields as automation triggers in the way that would make basic inbox triage possible without AI involvement.

What are you trying to achieve

What are you trying to achieve:
We need to route and manage incoming tickets automatically based on common email metadata. Specific use cases include:

Auto-assign tickets to the correct agent or team when the subject line contains specific keywords (e.g., “Order #”, “Return Request”, “Trade Account”)
Auto-close or deprioritize tickets that come from known informational/no-reply senders (e.g., system-generated emails we monitor but don’t action unless someone replies)
Route by domain — e.g., tickets from a known vendor domain always go to a specific queue

These are standard “filter + action” recipes in platforms like Zendesk, Freshdesk, Help Scout, and Front. We’d like to see them available as simple automation recipes in Monday Service — something like: “When email is received → If Subject contains [text] → Then assign to [agent]” or “If From address contains [@domain.com] → Then close ticket.” No AI parsing should be required for this level of conditional logic.
